concerns.text: Research.md states 'observational simulation study' and 'associational, not causal' but the design manipulates context condition (full vs limited) as an experimental factor. ANOVA with manipulated factor supports causal claims within the simulation. This inconsistency undermines methodological clarity.

Specialization index and retrieval efficiency are 'both derived from fact-contribution distributions' and may be 'definitionally related'. ANOVA treating them as independent factors violates assumption of orthogonal predictors. Need orthogonal validation or combined metric approach.

All primary datasets (Hanabi, CoQA) lack verified sources; implementation relies on synthetic cue/fact generation. Synthetic data may not capture transactive-memory dynamics complexity, threatening construct validity of both metrics. Need validation against real multi-agent interaction data.

Scaling analysis uses only 3 data points (3, 5, 7 agents) for power-law fitting. While acknowledged as underpowered, this cannot support claims about sublinear scaling laws (N^0.85). Consider reframing as exploratory trend analysis rather than power-law hypothesis test.
